name: PSideFlow shows empty-state copy when no payments have landed yet
description: Fresh account on /payments — every weekly bucket is zero. The cash-flow card must NOT draw a flat horizontal line (which reads as a chart bug). It should swap to an empty-state message: 'Nothing's landed yet — once payments roll in, the shape lights up.'

persona.name: Marcus Lee
persona.context: Hasn't billed anyone yet. Curious what /payments looks like before any money lands.

steps.expect: Hero subtitle handles the all-zero case (no awkward 'You've landed $0 this month' phrasing)
Title 'Cash-flow shape' + subtitle 'Last 12 weeks · nothing yet'; body reads 'Nothing's landed yet — once payments roll in, the shape lights up.'; NO svg path is drawn
No thin red line at the bottom of the chart area; no zero-area gradient fill

evaluationCriteria.check: Card height matches the data-bearing variant so the right-rail rhythm doesn't shift later
Reads as 'waiting' not 'broken' — the copy promises something will happen
PSideFlow's hasData branch is the source of truth; no dead SVG fragments leak through
Empty-state body is a plain <div> with normal contrast; not aria-hidden
